_comment: Branch ruleset for `master`. Imported once via `gh api repos/amiwrpremium/go-derive/rulesets --method POST --input .github/rulesets/branch-master.json`. Update in place: `gh api repos/.../rulesets/<id> --method PUT --input ...`. The ruleset supersedes the legacy branch-protection block in `.github/settings.yml`. Required-check `context` values are the GitHub-published check names — they match per-job names from .github/workflows/{ci,lint,codeql,gosec,extra-lint,license-check,dependency-review,trivy,gitleaks,trufflehog,semgrep,pr-title}.yml. Note the `enforce SHA pinning` check from pin-check.yml is intentionally NOT in this list: pin-check.yml has a paths filter (only runs when .github/workflows/** or .github/actions/** change), so under strict_required_status_checks_policy it would block every release PR (which doesn't touch workflow files). pin-check still gates workflow-changing PRs because it runs on those events; it's just not a per-PR required check. Conventional-commit format on the squash commit is covered by pr-title.yml (validates the PR title) plus `settings.yml`'s `squash_merge_commit_title: PR_TITLE` — the GitHub-Enterprise-only `commit_message_pattern` rule is intentionally absent because it requires GHAS. The pull_request rule has required_approving_review_count: 0 and require_code_owner_review: false because the project is solo-maintained — there's no reviewer pool other than the author. Changes still must go through a PR (not direct push), and status checks + signing remain the substantive quality gate. When/if the project grows past one maintainer, raise the review count and flip code-owner review back on.
name: master-branch-protection

bypass_actors._comment: Repo admin role — GitHub's built-in admin role ID is 5. Lets the maintainer push emergency hotfixes directly when CI gates aren't appropriate (e.g. broken master, security patch). Day-to-day changes still go through PR.
